problem_statement: Mfidel atomicity is enforced in local Python runtime tests, but cross-runtime SDK and kernel drift can still occur without a schema-backed receipt that records substrate digests, grid bounds, exact-preservation witnesses, no-normalization proof refs, and pending TypeScript/Rust evidence gaps.
success_criteria: MfidelSubstrateConformanceReceipt validates against a public JSON schema and Foundation Mode example
MfidelSubstrateConformanceReceipt records canonical grid bounds, vector dimension, known empty slots, overlay exceptions, and sound-only overlay metadata
MfidelSubstrateConformanceReceipt verifies local Python runtime digests for the substrate grid, legacy matrix view, and Mfidel contracts

MfidelSubstrateConformanceReceipt rejects Unicode normalization, decomposition, recomposition, root-letter modeling, consonant/vowel splitting, structural overlay decomposition, and terminal closure claims
MfidelSubstrateConformanceReceipt keeps TypeScript and Rust SDK/kernel bindings in AwaitingEvidence until digests and exact-preservation fixtures exist
MfidelSubstrateConformanceReceipt is covered by a focused validator, root test, protocol manifest entry, proof coverage matrix entry, and CI hook
non_goals: no lossy transliteration as canonical identity
constraints: each fidel remains an atomic symbol
grid reference remains f[row][col] with row in [1,34] and col in [1,8]
row 17 remains sound overlay metadata, not structural decomposition
cross_runtime_closure_claimed remains false in the Foundation example
TypeScript and Rust bindings remain AwaitingEvidence until local evidence exists
